name: Job offer with upfront payment
description: An unsolicited 'job offer,' often work-from-home, that requires you to pay for equipment, training, or to cash a check and send part of it back.
example: Congratulations! You've been selected for a remote data entry position paying $35/hour. To get started, we'll mail you a check for $2,500 for equipment. Deposit it and wire $1,800 to our supplier.
red_flags: You didn't apply for this job
Salary is unusually high for the described work
Requires depositing a check and sending money back (check will bounce later)
Communication is by text or generic email, not a company domain
